prompt: A massive layered glacier floating low in the sky directly above a small coastal village, visibly melting and breaking apart. Streams of meltwater fall from the glacier like rain and flow into the sea below, causing the ocean level around the village to rise. Several houses are already deeply submerged, some are halfway underwater, and a few remain barely above the rising waves, clearly showing the progression of flooding. The glacier contains visible horizontal ice layers and fractures that suggest accumulated time and gradual loss. Viewpoint from sea level among the flooded houses looking upward toward the melting glacier, creating a sense of scale and looming threat. Documentary-style environmental illustration suitable for a serious climate magazine cover, natural cold daylight, muted colors, textured ice and turbulent water, realistic atmosphere, emotionally powerful but grounded composition. Single frame, minimal background, clean composition, no text, no charts, no infographic elements, no labels. aspect ratio: 1:1 resolution: at least 1024x1024 style: editorial illustration / documentary realism NEGATIVE PROMPT: hourglass, bar charts, pie charts, line graphs, scatter plots, numbers, labels, text overlays, infographic icons, split panels, multi-frame layout, dramatic fantasy lighting, glowing effects, neon colors, sci-fi style, cartoon style, exaggerated fantasy landscapes
dataset_name: Global Ice Sheet and Glacier Mass Balance
insight: Global glaciers and ice sheets are losing hundreds of billions of tons of ice each year, contributing to rising sea levels that threaten coastal communities.
